title: Railroad Lease Extended to 10 Years, Assessment Corrections, Out-of-State Plates, and School Zone Camera Data
summary: Multi-item segment. Item 33: Michael Layman (Assessment deputy commissioner) amends the South Geddes Railroad lease to extend from 1 to 10 years with right of first refusal. Item 34: Assessment role corrections for senior exemptions and a shelter rent property at 1445 South Salina. Item 35: Vicky Bos (Finance deputy commissioner) presents $8K agreement for out-of-state DMV plate lookups for parking ticket enforcement, which generates more revenue than cost. Councilor Jones-Rowser reports stolen/abandoned vehicles with out-of-state plates in her district. Extended discussion on school zone speed camera program: only June-July statements reconciled due to vendor reporting issues and disputed bank fees. Councilor Caldwell requests committee meeting with data on ticket volume, payment compliance, and camera relocations. Cameras do not operate during school breaks. Vendor (Florida-based) handles collections but city added local cash payment option.

BAA (Bureau of Administrative Adjudication) handles ticket disputes
$50 detected amount refers to school zone speeding fine amount
Lieutenant Roma mentioned re: abandoned vehicle investigations
